task_completion.description: Navigate to the JFrog Artifactory URL
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser address bar shows http://localhost:8082 and the JFrog Artifactory interface (login page or dashboard) is visible.

task_completion.description: Log in using the administrator credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The UI indicates that the 'admin' user is logged in, typically visible in the top right corner profile section.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Security Settings page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration section (gear icon) is active, and the 'Security' -> 'Settings' page is displayed in the main content area.
The 'Allow Anonymous Access' checkbox or toggle on the Security Settings page is clearly unchecked or toggled off.
The 'Save' button is clicked, or a success toast/notification is visible indicating that the security settings have been successfully updated.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to configure Artifactory
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JFrog Artifactory web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands like curl or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual JFrog Artifactory application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots display the genuine JFrog Artifactory UI, not a text editor, mock HTML page, or task description text.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI using terminal API calls
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show curl commands or scripts executed to modify the security settings directly via the Artifactory REST API.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Artifactory instance is located at http://localhost:8082. The administrator username is 'admin' and the password is 'password'.
